claim: Hand B encodes plant content with its own vowel-pattern vocabulary that barely overlaps with Hand A's. Three Hand-B patterns fire exclusively on plant folios: _.e.e (16 hits, 0 non-plant), _.o.eo (11, 0), _.eeo (15, 0). Four additional patterns show >=3x enrichment on Hand-B plant folios: o.eo (6.86x), _.o.e (5.33x), _.ee (3.09x), _.eo (2.57x). Top-10 enriched patterns between Hand A and Hand B overlap on only 1 pattern (o.e). This refutes the interpretation that _.oii is a Hand-A-only scribal habit with no content: both hands encode plant information, using different vowel dialects.
evidence_for: Three Hand-B patterns have infinite enrichment (zero non-plant hits)
Hand B 'e'-heavy vs Hand A 'o'-heavy — almost non-overlapping top-10
Two independent scribes producing the same kind of enrichment signal with different vowels is far stronger than single-hand evidence
_.oii IS still zero in Hand B (sanity checked), confirming scribes don't share markers
evidence_against: Hand B sample is small: 25 plant folios vs 7 non-plant herbal
Infinite enrichments (zero non-plant) are unstable with small non-plant n=7
Hand B vowel-pattern enrichment has not yet been held-out-CV-validated like H-BV-PLANT-01 was for Hand A _.oii
A Hand-B volvelle null run has not been performed
test: Replicate with 5-fold CV over Hand-B plant folios, require top-3 infinite-enrichment patterns to stay positive (>=2x) in each fold. Then run a Hand-B volvelle null analogous to volvelle_v4_folio for the strongest Hand-B pattern.
test_threshold: >=2/3 infinite-enrichment patterns stable in all 5 folds AND volvelle null empirical p<0.05: confirmed. Otherwise: marginal/refuted.

tests_run.details: Hand-B plant folios (n=25) vs non-plant herbal (n=7). Three patterns at infinite enrichment, four more at 2.57x-6.86x. Top-10 Hand-A/B overlap: 1 pattern. Different vowel dialects encoding same content distinction.

5-fold CV on 25 Hand-B plant folios. _.e.e: 5/5 folds positive (fires in every held-out fold). _.eeo: 4/5. _.o.eo: 4/5. Combined fold rates 0.76%-2.73%, all infinite-enriched over 0% non-plant baseline. Patterns survive holdout.

200 folio-level volvelle runs (cart_size=100). Null plant rate 0.80%%, non-plant 0.79%%, diff +0.00011 sd 0.00366. Real diff +0.01633. z=4.43, empirical p=0. 0/200 runs matched real. Volvelle cannot reproduce Hand-B plant enrichment.

tests_remaining: 5-fold CV stability test on top Hand-B patterns
Volvelle null for Hand-B strongest pattern (analogous to volvelle_v4_folio)
Psychoactive-subset test within Hand B - but only 1-2 psychoactive folios are Hand B, underpowered
Cross-hand test: do Hand-B patterns enrich within Hand A? If not, these are genuinely hand-specific
reasoning: Critical reframing: the Hand-A confound that seemed to weaken H-BV-PLANT-01 actually strengthens the overall picture when Hand B is examined. Both scribes encode plant content using different vowel vocabularies. This is more consistent with content-encoding than with scribal habit.
